category_title: Preschoolers who talk out of turn during group activities
scenario_title: Talking out of turn during large group circle time
minimal_context: Sybil is participating in large group circle time where the class is taking turns sharing stories.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Welcome to circle time, everyone. Today we are going to take turns sharing a story about our weekend.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I went to the zoo with my family and we saw the monkeys and they were swinging everywhere!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, let's let Sarah tell her story now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: And then after the monkeys we saw the lions and one of them roared super loud at us!

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: But I have to tell you about the elephants too! They were spraying water and my sisters got all wet!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Welcome to circle time, everyone. Today we are going to take turns sharing a story about our weekend.

STUDENT: I went to the zoo with my family and we saw the monkeys and they were swinging everywhere!

TUTOR: Okay, let's let Sarah tell her story now.

STUDENT: And then after the monkeys we saw the lions and one of them roared super loud at us!

TUTOR: Please wait, Sybil.

STUDENT: But I have to tell you about the elephants too! They were spraying water and my sisters got all wet!

task.content: Interacting with peers and adults during classroom activities; specifically telling stories and talking where turn-taking in conversation is required
task.learning_goal: have the opportunity to listen and learn from friends and adults in the classroom

context.environment: Large group circle time at school
context.materials: pictures to remind Sybil to raise her hand before speaking and wait her turn to talk

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When participating in group activities where turn-taking in conversation is required
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Sybil does not raise her hand or signal the need to speak before talking; talks over a friend or adult; and does not pause to hear her friend’s or adult’s response.
related_quotes: Sybil talks so much that the other children do not have time to share.

behavior_patterns.condition: During large group circle time at school
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: talking out of turn as much as 80% of the time during group activities
related_quotes: talks over a friend or adult
does not pause to hear her friend’s or adult’s response
